title: Non-record: Cache LM + LoRA TTT (negative result on cache, positive on TTT)
body:
## Summary

Non-record submission exploring two eval-time techniques with zero artifact cost:

- **LoRA TTT**: Per-document rank-8 LoRA adaptation on Q/V/lm_head with Adam lr=0.01, 256-token chunks. Gives -0.003 BPB improvement over sliding window.
- **Unigram Cache LM** (Grave et al. 2017): Interpolate model softmax with decayed per-document token frequency distribution. **Negative result**: λ=0.02 hurts BPB by +0.002 on FineWeb.

### Results

| Eval Mode | val_bpb | Δ |
|-----------|---------|---|
| Sliding window stride=128 | 1.2562 | — |
| + LoRA TTT | 1.2529 | -0.003 |
| + LoRA TTT + Cache LM | 1.2544 | +0.002 (cache hurts) |

### Why cache LM fails on FineWeb

Grave et al. 2017 showed 12-31% perplexity reduction on WikiText/PTB, but FineWeb web text is too diverse — documents are shorter and more varied, so the unigram cache doesn't accumulate enough signal before document boundaries reset it. The technique likely needs continuous cache (hidden state similarity) rather than unigram counts to work on web text.

### Setup

- Model: v8192 7L GQA + MTP k=2 α=0.3, tied embeddings
- Training: 2000 steps on 1xA100 (1/10th of full budget, no torch.compile)
- Compressed size: 15.14 MB (int8+zlib)

## Test plan

- [x] Training completes and produces valid model
- [x] Sliding window eval reproduces expected BPB
- [x] LoRA TTT confirms -0.003 BPB improvement
- [x] Cache LM runs without errors (result: negative)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
